description:
Extreme disappointment and mental distress to formally complain about the unjustified harassment, delay, and mishandling of my cashless hospitalization claim under ICICI Lombard Elevate Policy No. 100003815100, with a Sum Insured of ₹25 Lakhs, purchased on 29 April 2025 after paying a premium of ₹22,816/-.

Hospitalization Details
I was admitted to Max Hospital, Noida on 14 January 2026 due to severe abdominal pain and multiple episodes of vomiting. The hospital raised a cashless pre-authorization request on the same day.

Sequence of Events and Harassment Faced
On the night of admission, I received a message from ICICI Lombard stating that admission-related papers and test reports were required.

However, the hospital TPA confirmed that they received no such document request from ICICI Lombard.

Shockingly, the very next morning at 9:00 AM, my claim was marked “Rejected due to non-receipt of documents”.

Upon calling customer care, I was advised to submit documents, which the hospital TPA duly submitted by 1:00 PM the same day.

Despite this, ICICI Lombard repeatedly raised additional queries—first for past medical records, then for medical tests conducted in June 2025, all of which were arranged and submitted.

Subsequently, a third-party verification agent visited my hospital room, took photographs, made me and my wife fill multiple forms, and obtained a declaration confirming that this was the first time I had experienced such severe pain.

Even after this, ICICI Lombard again raised the same repetitive query for past medical records, despite already receiving declarations and confirmations from my wife and the hospital TPA.

Forced to Pay from My Own Pocket
From 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM, despite continuous follow-ups and full cooperation, ICICI Lombard neither approved nor formally rejected the claim and continued raising the same queries repeatedly.

Due to unbearable pain and mental stress, I was left with no option but to pay ₹50,000/- from my own pocket to proceed with treatment.

Serious Concerns
Even after paying a high premium and opting for a ₹25 lakh sum insured, a basic claim of ₹50,000/- was not honored.

This raises serious concerns about the credibility, intent, and reliability of ICICI Lombard’s cashless claim process.

Had my treatment required lakhs of rupees, this deliberate delay and harassment could have placed my life at serious risk.

This experience has completely shattered #viral #trending #family #friends #viralvideo #vlog #reels  my trust in ICICI Lombard. What I faced was not a service issue but systematic harassment, seemingly intended to discourage and exhaust the policyholder.

Pooja ji sahi kaha aapne main 4 saal se star health Insurance policy chala raha tha. This policy was taken by getting services of an employee of star health I. E may be she could either be in sales team. 
In September 2025 I came to know through an article or video on YouTube that star health is not meeting it's claims on time or rejecting them and is planning to stop cashless health claim facility. 
I dropped what's aaap messaage to my insurance star employee agent in sept 2025 to enquire from her on this issue
Reply from her was very far away even my dropped message was not seen and even I was not called for my query. I was shocked to see this support. 
So when my premium due date came in end of December 2025 I was called by same employee at my cell no and I did not picked call knowingly . After few days star people called me and asked for premium due I told them I am disappointed and will not continue policy. When I asked for where about and complain for that agent employee from whom I had taken policy them star people told me that she had already left job in oct 2025.

Is this not carelessness from people there. 
God forbid if any emergency comes we have no communication channels whole year. No list of hospital provided no support. 

So finally I decided one way of this solution.. I hope it will work well for suffering patients.. 

Just instead of paying premium to insurance companies just pay that premium or start a sip in equity mutual fund and dedicate that one fund for your health emergency 

God forbid of any emergency arise then that fund helps and may be patients need not run from pilar to post or at mercy of insurance companies. 

I have stopped my health insurance policy now and will never start it and pay any premium to health insurance companies because in our country products are sold but after sale services are very poor. Today health sector really news reforms as education and health are truly out of reach for common man
.

comments.text: I understand that ICICI Lombard did not specify the document that they were asking you to submit for cashless authorisation. This could be a strong ground for taking the fight further. Please keep copy of all communications between ICICI Lombard and you to establish that they didn't specify the document sought for. Your right step will be to pursue the claim through Insurance Ombudsman, which you said you have already processed. In case you don't succeed, pull the insurer to the jurisdictional consumer forum (though the process is likely to be time and patience consuming). Don't terminate the policy immediately but wait until sixty days before the next renewal date and seek porting to another insurer. Meanwhile, reasearch thoroughly to gather information of a right insurance company on which you can rely for further continuance. You can't just take a chance with any other insurer, pay the premium for years and get deceived at the time of a claim and then again take up the process of fighting and port once again. Meanwhile, remain healthy, both physically and mentally, so necessity of claim never arise. Thanks for exposing ICICI Lombard for prospective insureds.
